sections.label: Section 1
sections.original:
Δήμητερ ἑστιοῦχʼ Ἐλευσῖνος χθονὸς
τῆσδʼ , οἵ τε ναοὺς ἔχετε πρόσπολοι θεᾶς ,
εὐδαιμονεῖν με Θησέα τε παῖδʼ ἐμὸν
πόλιν τʼ Ἀθηνῶν τήν τε Πιτθέως χθόνα ,
sections.translation: O Demeter , guardian of this Eleusinian land , and ye servants of the goddess who attend her fane , grant happiness to me and my son Theseus , to the city of Athens and the country of Pittheus ,

sections.label: Section 5
sections.original:
ἐν ᾗ με θρέψας ὀλβίοις ἐν δώμασιν
Αἴθραν πατὴρ δίδωσι τῷ Πανδίονος
Αἰγεῖ δάμαρτα , Λοξίου μαντεύμασιν .
ἐς τάσδε γὰρ βλέψασʼ ἐπηυξάμην τάδε
γραῦς , αἳ λιποῦσαι δώματʼ Ἀργείας χθονὸς
sections.translation: wherein my father reared me , Aethra , in a happy home , and gave me in marriage to Aegeus , Pandion ’ s son , according to the oracle of Loxias . This prayer I make , when I behold these aged dames , who , leaving their homes in Argos ,

sections.label: Section 10
sections.original:
ἱκτῆρι θαλλῷ προσπίτνουσʼ ἐμὸν γόνυ ,
πάθος παθοῦσαι δεινόν · ἀμφὶ γὰρ πύλας
Κάδμου θανόντων ἑπτὰ γενναίων τέκνων
ἄπαιδές εἰσιν , οὕς ποτʼ Ἀργείων ἄναξ
sections.translation: now throw themselves with suppliant branches at my knees in their awful trouble ; for around the gates of Cadmus have they lost their seven noble sons , whom on a day Adrastus , king of Argos , led thither ,

sections.label: Section 15
sections.original:
Ἄδραστος ἤγαγʼ , Οἰδίπου παγκληρίας
μέρος κατασχεῖν φυγάδι Πολυνείκει θέλων
γαμβρῷ . νεκροὺς δὲ τοὺς ὀλωλότας δορὶ
θάψαι θελουσῶν τῶνδε μητέρων χθονὶ
εἴργουσιν οἱ κρατοῦντες οὐδʼ ἀναίρεσιν
δοῦναι θέλουσι , νόμιμʼ ἀτίζοντες θεῶν .
sections.translation: eager to secure for exiled Polynices , his son-in-law , a share in the heritage of Oedipus ; so now their mothers would bury in the grave the dead , whom the spear hath slain , but the victors prevent them and will not allow them to take up the corpses , spurning Heaven ’ s laws .

sections.label: Section 20
sections.original:
κοινὸν δὲ φόρτον ταῖσδʼ ἔχων χρείας ἐμῆς
Ἄδραστος ὄμμα δάκρυσιν τέγγων ὅδε
κεῖται , τό τʼ ἔγχος τήν τε δυστυχεστάτην
στένων στρατείαν ἣν ἔπεμψεν ἐκ δόμων ·
ὅς μʼ ἐξοτρύνει παῖδʼ ἐμὸν πεῖσαι λιταῖς
sections.translation: Here lies Adrastus on the ground with streaming eye , sharing with them the burden of their prayer to me , and bemoaning the havoc of the sword and the sorry fate of the warriors whom he led from their homes . And he doth urge me use entreaty , to persuade my son

sections.label: Section 25
sections.original:
νεκρῶν κομιστὴν ἢ λόγοισιν ἢ δορὸς
ῥώμῃ γενέσθαι καὶ τάφου μεταίτιον ,
μόνον τόδʼ ἔργον προστιθεὶς ἐμῷ τέκνῳ
πόλει τʼ Ἀθηνῶν . τυγχάνω δʼ ὑπὲρ χθονὸς
ἀρότου προθύουσʼ , ἐκ δόμων ἐλθοῦσʼ ἐμῶν
sections.translation: to take up the dead and help to bury them , either by winning words or force of arms , laying on my son and on Athens this task alone . Now it chanced , that I had left my house and come to offer sacrifice on behalf of the earth ’ s crop

sections.label: Section 30
sections.original:
πρὸς τόνδε σηκόν , ἔνθα πρῶτα φαίνεται
φρίξας ὑπὲρ γῆς τῆσδε κάρπιμος στάχυς .
δεσμὸν δʼ ἄδεσμον τόνδʼ ἔχουσα φυλλάδος
μένω πρὸς ἁγναῖς ἐσχάραις δυοῖν θεαῖν
Κόρης τε καὶ Δήμητρος , οἰκτίρουσα μὲν
sections.translation: at this shrine , where first the fruitful corn showed its bristling shocks above the soil . And here at the holy altars of the twain goddesses , Demeter and her daughter , I wait , holding these sprays of foliage , a bond that bindeth not , in compassion for

sections.label: Section 35
sections.original:
πολιὰς ἄπαιδας τάσδε μητέρας τέκνων ,
σέβουσα δʼ ἱερὰ στέμματʼ . οἴχεται δέ μοι
κῆρυξ πρὸς ἄστυ δεῦρο Θησέα καλῶν ,
ὡς ἢ τὸ τούτων λυπρὸν ἐξέλῃ χθονός ,
ἢ τάσδʼ ἀνάγκας ἱκεσίους λύσῃ , θεοὺς
sections.translation: these childless mothers , hoary with age , and from reverence for the sacred fillets . To call Theseus hither is my herald to the city gone , that he may rid the land of that which grieveth them , or loose these my suppliant bonds ,

sections.label: Section 40
sections.original:
ὅσιόν τι δράσας · πάντα γὰρ διʼ ἀρσένων
γυναιξὶ πράσσειν εἰκός , αἵτινες σοφαί .
sections.translation: with pious observance of the gods ’ will ; for such as are discreet amongst women should in all cases invoke the aid of men .

sections.label: Section 1.42
sections.original:
— ἱκετεύω σε , γεραιά ,
γεραιῶν ἐκ στομάτων , πρὸς
sections.translation: At thy knees I fall , aged dame , and my old lips beseech thee ;

sections.label: Section 1.45
sections.original:
γόνυ πίπτουσα τὸ σόν ·
— ἄνομοι ⋯ — τέκνα λῦσαι . — φθιμένων
νεκύων οἳ καταλείπουσι μέλη
sections.translation: arise , rescue from the slain my children ’ s bodies , whose limbs , by death relaxed , are left a prey to savage mountain beasts ,

sections.label: Section 1.48
sections.original: θανάτῳ λυσιμελεῖ θηρσὶν ὀρείοισι βοράν ·
sections.translation: beholding the bitter tears which spring to my eyes

sections.label: Section 1.50
sections.original:
— ἐσιδοῦσʼ οἰκτρὰ μὲν ὄσσων
δάκρυʼ ἀμφὶ βλεφάροις , ῥυ-
σὰ δὲ σαρκῶν πολιᾶν
καταδρύμματα χειρῶν · τί γάρ ; ἃ
sections.translation: and my old wrinkled skin torn by my hands ; for what can I do else ? who never laid out my children dead within my halls , nor now behold their tombs heaped up with earth .

sections.label: Section 2.54
sections.original: φθιμένους παῖδας ἐμοὺς οὔτε δόμοις
sections.translation: Thou too , honoured lady , once a son didst bear ,

sections.label: Section 2.55
sections.original:
προθέμαν οὔτε τάφων χώματα γαίας ἐσορῶ .
— ἔτεκες καὶ σύ ποτʼ , ὦ πότνια , κοῦρον
φίλα ποιησαμένα λέ-
κτρα πόσει σῷ · μετά νυν
δὸς ἐμοὶ σᾶς διανοίας ,
sections.translation: crowning thy lord ’ s marriage with fond joy ; then share , O share with me thy mother ’ s feelings , in such measure as my sad heart grieves for my own dead sons ;

sections.label: Section 2.60
sections.original:
μετάδος δʼ , ὅσσον ἐπαλγῶ μελέα ʼγὼ
φθιμένων οὓς ἔτεκον ·
παράπεισον δὲ σόν , ὤ , λισσόμεθʼ , ἐλθεῖν
τέκνον Ἰσμηνὸν ἐμάν τʼ ἐς χέρα θεῖναι
sections.translation: and persuade thy son , whose aid we implore , to go unto the river Ismenus , there to place within my hapless arms the bodies of my children , slain in their prime and left without a tomb . [*]

sections.label: Section 2.63
sections.original:
νεκύων θαλερᾷ σώματʼ ἀλαίνοντʼ ἄταφα .
— ὁσίως οὔχ , ὑπʼ ἀνάγκας δὲ προπίπτου-
σα προσαιτοῦσʼ ἔμολον δε-
sections.translation: Though [*] not as piety enjoins , yet from sheer necessity I have come to the fire-crowned altars of the gods , falling on my knees with instant supplication ,

sections.label: Section 2.65
sections.original:
ξιπύρους θεῶν θυμέλας ·
ἔχομεν δʼ ἔνδικα , καὶ σοί
τι πάρεστι σθένος ὥστʼ εὐτεκνίᾳ δυσ-
τυχίαν τὰν παρʼ ἐμοὶ
καθελεῖν · οἰκτρὰ δὲ πάσχουσʼ ἱκετεύω
sections.translation: for my cause is just , and ’ tis in thy power , blest as thou art in thy children , to remove from me my woe ; so in my sore distress I do beseech thee of my misery place in my hands my son ’ s

sections.label: Section 2.70
sections.original: σὸν ἐμοὶ παῖδα ταλαίνᾳ ʼν χερὶ θεῖναι
sections.translation: dead body , that I may throw my arms about his hapless limbs .

sections.label: Section 3.71
sections.original:
νέκυν , ἀμφιβαλεῖν λυγρὰ μέλη παιδὸς ἐμοῦ .
— ἀγὼν ὅδʼ ἄλλος ἔρχεται γόων γόων
διάδοχος , ἀχοῦσι προσπόλων χέρες .
ἴτʼ ὦ ξυνῳδοὶ κτύποι ,
sections.translation: Behold a rivalry in sorrow ! woe takes up the tale of woe ; hark ! thy servants beat their breasts . Come ye who join the mourners ’ wail , come , O sympathetic band , to join the dance ,

sections.label: Section 3.75
sections.original:
ἴτʼ ὦ ξυναλγηδόνες ,
χορὸν τὸν Ἅιδας σέβει ,
διὰ παρῇδος ὄνυχα λευκὸν
αἱματοῦτε χρῶτά τε φόνιον ·
τὰ γὰρ φθιτῶν
τοῖς ὁρῶσι κόσμος .
sections.translation: which Hades honours ; let the [*] pearly nail be stained red , as it rends your cheeks , let your skin be streaked with gore ; for honours rendered to the dead are a credit [*] to the living .

sections.label: Section 3.79
sections.original: — ἄπληστος ἅδε μʼ ἐξάγει χάρις γόων
sections.translation: Sorrow ’ s charm doth drive me wild , insatiate , painful ,

sections.label: Section 3.80
sections.original:
πολύπονος , ὡς ἐξ ἀλιβάτου πέτρας
ὑγρὰ ῥέουσα σταγὼν
ἄπαυστος αἰεί · γόων ,
τὸ γὰρ θανόντων τέκνων
sections.translation: endless , even as the trickling stream that gushes from some steep rock ’ s face ; for ’ tis woman ’ s way to fall a-weeping o ’ er

sections.label: Section 3.85
sections.original:
ἐπιπονόν τι κατὰ γυναῖκας
ἐς γόους πέφυκε πάθος . ἒ ἔ .
θανοῦσα τῶνδʼ
sections.translation: the cruel calamity of children dead . Ah me ! would I could die and forget my anguish !

sections.label: Section 87
sections.original:
ἀλγέων λαθοίμαν .
τίνων γόων ἤκουσα καὶ στέρνων κτύπον
νεκρῶν τε θρήνους , τῶνδʼ ἀνακτόρων ἄπο
ἠχοῦς ἰούσης ; ὡς φόβος μʼ ἀναπτεροῖ
sections.translation: What is this lamentation that I hear , this beating of the breast , these dirges for the dead , with cries that echo from this shrine ? How fluttering fear disquiets me ,

sections.label: Section 90
sections.original:
μή μοί τι μήτηρ , ἣν μεταστείχω ποδί ,
χρονίαν ἀποῦσαν ἐκ δόμων ἔχῃ νέον .
ἔα ·
τί χρῆμα ; καινὰς ἐσβολὰς ὁρῶ λόγων ·
μητέρα γεραιὰν βωμίαν ἐφημένην
ξένους θʼ ὁμοῦ γυναῖκας , οὐχ ἕνα ῥυθμὸν
sections.translation: lest haply my mother have gotten some mischance , in quest of whom I come , for she hath been long absent from home . Ha ! what now ? A strange sight challenges my speech ; I see my aged mother sitting at the altar and stranger dames are with her , who in various note

sections.label: Section 95
sections.original:
κακῶν ἐχούσας · ἔκ τε γὰρ γερασμίων
ὄσσων ἐλαύνουσʼ οἰκτρὸν ἐς γαῖαν δάκρυ ,
κουραί τε καὶ πεπλώματʼ οὐ θεωρικά .
sections.translation: proclaim their woe ; from aged eyes the piteous tear is starting to the ground , their hair is shorn , their robes are not the robes of joy .

sections.label: Section 98
sections.original:
τί ταῦτα , μῆτερ ; σὸν τὸ μηνύειν ἐμοί ,
ἡμῶν δʼ ἀκούειν · προσδοκῶ τι γὰρ νέον .
sections.translation: What means it , mother ? ’ Tis thine to make it plain to me , mine to listen ; yea , for I expect some tidings strange .

sections.label: Section 100
sections.original:
ὦ παῖ , γυναῖκες αἵδε μητέρες τέκνων
τῶν κατθανόντων ἀμφὶ Καδμείας πύλας
ἑπτὰ στρατηγῶν · ἱκεσίοις δὲ σὺν κλάδοις
φρουροῦσί μʼ , ὡς δέδορκας , ἐν κύκλῳ , τέκνον .
sections.translation: My son , these are the mothers of those chieftains seven , who fell around the gates of Cadmus ’ town . With suppliant boughs they keep me prisoner , as thou seest , in their midst .

sections.label: Section 104
sections.original: τίς δʼ ὁ στενάζων οἰκτρὸν ἐν πύλαις ὅδε ;
sections.translation: And who is yonder man , that moaneth piteously in the gateway ?

sections.label: Section 105
sections.original: Ἄδραστος , ὡς λέγουσιν , Ἀργείων ἄναξ .
sections.translation: Adrastus , they inform me , king of Argos .

sections.label: Section 106
sections.original: οἱ δʼ ἀμφὶ τόνδε παῖδες ; ἦ τούτων τέκνα ;
sections.translation: Are those his children , those boys who stand round him ?

sections.label: Section 107
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ νεκρῶν τῶν ὀλωλότων κόροι .
sections.translation: Not his , but the sons of the fallen slain .

sections.label: Section 108
sections.original: τί γὰρ πρὸς ἡμᾶς ἦλθον ἱκεσίᾳ χερί ;
sections.translation: Why are they come to us , with suppliant hand outstretched ?

sections.label: Section 109
sections.original: οἶδʼ · ἀλλὰ τῶνδε μῦθος οὑντεῦθεν , τέκνον .
sections.translation: I know ; but ’ tis for them to tell their story , my son .

sections.label: Section 110
sections.original:
σὲ τὸν κατήρη χλανιδίοις ἀνιστορῶ .
λέγʼ ἐκκαλύψας κρᾶτα καὶ πάρες γόον ·
πέρας γὰρ οὐδὲν μὴ διὰ γλώσσης ἰόν .
sections.translation: To thee , in thy mantle muffled , I address my inquiries ; unveil thy head , let lamentation be , and speak ; for naught can be achieved save through the utterance of thy tongue . [*]

sections.label: Section 113
sections.original:
ὦ καλλίνικε γῆς Ἀθηναίων ἄναξ ,
Θησεῦ , σὸς ἱκέτης καὶ πόλεως ἥκω σέθεν .
sections.translation: Victorious prince of the Athenian realm , Theseus , to thee and to thy city I , a suppliant , come .

sections.label: Section 115
sections.original: τί χρῆμα θηρῶν καὶ τίνος χρείαν ἔχων ;
sections.translation: What seekest thou ? What need is thine ?

sections.label: Section 116
sections.original: οἶσθʼ ἣν στρατείαν ἐστράτευσʼ ὀλεθρίαν .
sections.translation: Dost know how I did lead an expedition to its ruin ?

sections.label: Section 117
sections.original: οὐ γάρ τι σιγῇ διεπέρασας Ἑλλάδα .
sections.translation: Assuredly ; thou didst not pass through Hellas , all in silence .

sections.label: Section 118
sections.original: ἐνταῦθʼ ἀπώλεσʼ ἄνδρας Ἀργείων ἄκρους .
sections.translation: There I lost the pick of Argos ’ sons .

sections.label: Section 119
sections.original: τοιαῦθʼ ὁ τλήμων πόλεμος ἐξεργάζεται .
sections.translation: These are the results of that unhappy war .

sections.label: Section 120
sections.original: τούτους θανόντας ἦλθον ἐξαιτῶν πόλιν .
sections.translation: I went and craved their bodies from Thebes .

sections.label: Section 121
sections.original: κήρυξιν Ἑρμοῦ πίσυνος , ὡς θάψῃς νεκρούς ;
sections.translation: Didst thou rely on heralds , Hermes ’ servants , in order to bury them ?

sections.label: Section 122
sections.original: κἄπειτά γʼ οἱ κτανόντες οὐκ ἐῶσί με .
sections.translation: I did ; and even then their slayers said me nay .

sections.label: Section 123
sections.original: τί γὰρ λέγουσιν , ὅσια χρῄζοντος σέθεν ;
sections.translation: Why , what say they to thy just request ?

sections.label: Section 124
sections.original: τί δʼ ; εὐτυχοῦντες οὐκ ἐπίστανται φέρειν .
sections.translation: Say ! Success makes them forget how to bear their fortune .

sections.label: Section 125
sections.original: ξύμβουλον οὖν μʼ ἐπῆλθες ; ἢ τίνος χάριν ;
sections.translation: Art come to me then for counsel ? or wherefore ?

sections.label: Section 126
sections.original: κομίσαι σε , Θησεῦ , παῖδας Ἀργείων θέλων .
sections.translation: With the wish that thou , O Theseus , shouldst recover the sons of the Argives .

sections.label: Section 127
sections.original: τὸ δʼ Ἄργος ἡμῖν ποῦ ʼστιν ; ἢ κόμποι μάτην ;
sections.translation: Where is your Argos now ? were its vauntings all in vain ?

sections.label: Section 128
sections.original: σφαλέντες οἰχόμεσθα . πρὸς σὲ δʼ ἥκομεν .
sections.translation: Defeat and ruin are our lot . To thee for aid we come .

sections.label: Section 129
sections.original: ἰδίᾳ δοκῆσάν σοι τόδʼ ἢ πάσῃ πόλει ;
sections.translation: this thy own private resolve , or the wish of all the city ?

sections.label: Section 130
sections.original: πάντες σʼ ἱκνοῦνται Δαναΐδαι θάψαι νεκρούς .
sections.translation: The sons of Danaus , one and all , implore thee to bury the dead .

sections.label: Section 131
sections.original: ἐκ τοῦ δʼ ἐλαύνεις ἑπτὰ πρὸς Θήβας λόχους ;
sections.translation: Why didst lead thy seven armies against Thebes ?

sections.label: Section 132
sections.original: δισσοῖσι γαμβροῖς τήνδε πορσύνων χάριν .
sections.translation: To confer that favour on the husbands of my daughters twain .

sections.label: Section 133
sections.original: τῷ δʼ ἐξέδωκας παῖδας Ἀργείων σέθεν ;
sections.translation: To which of the Argives didst thou give thy daughters in marriage ?

sections.label: Section 134
sections.original: οὐκ ἐγγενῆ συνῆψα κηδείαν δόμοις .
sections.translation: I made no match for them with kinsmen of my family .

sections.label: Section 135
sections.original: ἀλλὰ ξένοις ἔδωκας Ἀργείας κόρας ;
sections.translation: What ! didst give Argive maids to foreign lords ?

sections.label: Section 136
sections.original: Τυδεῖ γε Πολυνείκει τε τῷ Θηβαιγενεῖ .
sections.translation: Yea , to Tydeus , and to Polynices , who was Theban-born .

sections.label: Section 137
sections.original: τίνʼ εἰς ἔρωτα τῆσδε κηδείας μολών ;
sections.translation: What induced thee to select this alliance ?
